key_insight: H-Res adapts frozen Transformer associative memories by steering token dynamics on the activation manifold, rather than changing weights or adding prompt tokens.
engineering_relevance: This offers a low-overhead way to specialize large models while preserving their learned memory structure and avoiding the stability costs of direct parameter edits.
method: Learn a state-dependent residual vector field that shapes the model’s effective energy landscape.
Steer trajectories into task-specific attractor basins without increasing sequence length.
Preserve the foundation model’s attention entropy while encouraging neural collapse in the adapted space.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 26% better performance than global weight modification on associative retrieval tasks and improved scalability on structured domains.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurately learning the residual steering field, so implementation quality and generalization across tasks may be sensitive to optimization and domain shift.
paper_title: Parallel Manifold Steering: Efficient Adaptation of Large Associative Memories via Residual Energy Shaping
